title: Tender Smoky Roasted Irish Spring Onions Gratin
description: A winey, energizing Irish dish featuring spring onions roasted to perfection with binchotan charcoal, served as a side or main course.
instructions: Trim the spring onions, leaving the roots intact. Cut the onions in half lengthwise and place them in a baking dish.
In a small saucepan, melt 1 tablespoon of butter over medium heat. Add the chopped binchotan charcoal and cook for 2-3 minutes, until fragrant.
Sprinkle the flour over the onions and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Gradually pour in the milk, whisking continuously to prevent lumps. Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes, until thickened.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the grated Irish cheddar cheese, chopped thyme, salt, and black pepper.
Pour the cheese sauce over the spring onions and dot with the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter.
Transfer the baking dish to the preheated oven and roast for 25-30 minutes, until the onions are tender and the top is golden brown.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes before serving.
